featuredProblem.title: Burst Balloons
featuredProblem.problemStatement: Given n balloons, each assigned a number, burst them in any order. Bursting balloon i earns nums[i-1] * nums[i] * nums[i+1] coins. After it's burst, its neighbors become adjacent. Find the maximum coins collectable.
featuredProblem.approach: The key inversion is to think about which balloon to burst last within a range, not which to burst first. Define dp[i][j] as the maximum coins obtainable from bursting all balloons strictly between index i and j, with i and j as fixed boundary sentinels. For each candidate k as the last balloon burst in range (i, j), the coins earned are nums[i] * nums[k] * nums[j] plus dp[i][k] + dp[k][j]. The last-burst framing makes the subproblems independent because k's neighbors at burst time are always i and j — no ambiguity from earlier bursts.
featuredProblem.keyInsight: Reframe the problem from first-burst to last-burst. The last balloon popped in any range always has fixed neighbors (the range boundaries), making subproblems cleanly independent and enabling interval DP.
